title: Ashlar Agency Theme
description: DTCG-aligned theme document. Each agency theme lives in its own JSON file under packages/cli/themes/ (or in a downstream theme registry). The CLI's theme loader scans the directory and validates every file against this schema before compilation.

name.description: Stable theme identifier used as the data-ashlar-theme attribute value.

description.description: One-sentence description shown in theme pickers and contributor docs.

provenance.description: Human-review metadata describing whether this theme is source-derived, official, or unverified. Source-derived themes use public agency or government design sources but are not agency endorsements.

status.description: Source confidence. `official-adapter` is reserved for themes reviewed or published by the agency or an authorized design-system owner.

reviewedAt.description: Date this theme's source mapping was last checked, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

reviewedBy.description: Person, organization, or review role that checked the source mapping.

summary.description: Plain-language claim boundary for this theme's relationship to its sources.

sources.description: Primary agency or government design-system sources used to derive this theme. These links travel with generated theme JSON so downstream teams can audit provenance.

label.description: Short source name shown in theme provenance UIs.

url.description: Canonical public URL for the agency design-system or brand source.

note.description: Short note describing which tokens or design constraints came from this source.

retrievedAt.description: Date this public source was last checked, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

tokenPaths.description: Ashlar DTCG token paths directly informed by this source.

order.description: Sort hint; lower numbers appear first. Default theme is 0.

modes.description: Mode-specific token overrides. Currently only `dark` is defined; additional modes (high-contrast, forced-colors) may follow.
